title: tabebuia lopezii
intro: Tabebuia lopezii, also known as "Paya" or "Tree of Life," is a type of flowering plant native to Central and South America, belonging to the Bignoniaceae family. The medicinal properties of Tabebuia lopezii lie in its bark, which contains a number of bioactive compounds, including lapachol, a naphthoquinone with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Tabebuia lopezii are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit potential anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of Tabebuia lopezii typically involve extracting its lapachol content through processes such as solvent extraction or decoction, often in the form of teas, capsules, or tinctures.
